serviceMoment.title: My Internet Scan
serviceMoment.subtitle: How is your WiFi at home?

intro.positiveTitle: Great to hear things are going well!
intro.positiveSubtitle: If you ever run into an issue, you can always come back here and we'll help sort it out.
intro.negativeSubtitle: Let's take a look and see what might be going on with your connection.

preScan.title: What we'll check
preScan.subtitle: The scan takes a few seconds and will analyse three key areas of your connection.

scanning.title: Scanning your connection
facts: The first WiFi standard was released in 1997 and had a max speed of 2 Mbps.
WiFi signals travel at the speed of light — about 300,000 km/s.
The average household has over 25 connected devices.
Your microwave oven operates on the same frequency as your WiFi router.
The name 'WiFi' doesn't actually stand for anything!
A single WiFi router can theoretically cover about 50 metres indoors.
Streaming a 4K movie uses about 7 GB per hour.
The world record for the longest WiFi connection is over 380 km.

retestWarning.title: Before you retest
retestWarning.subtitle: Our diagnostic data is not updated in real time. After a fix has been applied, it can take up to one week for new results to appear.
retestWarning.warning: Running a scan now may show outdated information and give an incorrect result.

allClear.noIssuesSubtitle: Your connection looks healthy from our end.

mismatch.title: We hear you
mismatch.subtitle: Your experience doesn't match what our systems are showing. We understand how frustrating that can be, and we want to help.
mismatch.reasonBody: Our diagnostic systems monitor your connection remotely, but they can't always capture the full picture of what happens inside your home.
whyReasons: WiFi interference from nearby devices or neighbours
Physical obstacles (walls, floors) weakening the signal
Issues with specific devices that our systems can't detect
Temporary slowdowns that resolve before our checks run
mismatch.suggestionBody: Try the troubleshooting steps on the next screen, or contact our support team for personalised help.

mismatchActions.title: Let's make it right
mismatchActions.subtitle: Here are some things you can try to improve your experience.

positiveMismatch.title: Good to hear — but we noticed something
positiveMismatch.subtitle: You're happy with your WiFi, but our systems have picked up a potential issue. It may not affect you yet — but it's worth knowing about.
whyReasons: Early signs of degradation that haven't become noticeable
Intermittent signal instability during off-peak hours
Hardware indicators that suggest future problems
Network-level patterns that may worsen over time
positiveMismatch.suggestionBody: You can ignore this for now, or take a look at the issue to prevent problems down the road.

positiveMismatchActions.title: Possible actions
positiveMismatchActions.subtitle: Since everything feels fine on your end, here's what you can do.

modemOffline.title: No internet connection
modemOffline.subtitle: We detect that you have no internet connection.
modemOffline.turnOnDesc: Make sure your modem is powered on and the power light is solid.
modemOffline.restartDesc: Unplug your modem, wait 10 seconds, then plug it back in.
modemOffline.waitInfo: Wait at least 5 minutes before retesting your connection.

networkIncident.title: Poor or no connectivity
networkIncident.subtitle: We detect that you have poor or no connectivity. The most probable cause is a network issue that we are already aware of and working on.
networkIncident.waitForTechDesc: Our team is already aware and working on resolving this issue.

wifiHelp.title: WiFi troubleshooting
wifiHelp.subtitle: Try one of these options to improve your connection.
wifiHelp.diyTipsDesc: Learn how to fix common WiFi issues
wifiHelp.orderBoosterDesc: Extend your coverage with a booster

modemReboot.title: Restart your modem
modemReboot.rebootInfo: The reboot will take approximately 5 minutes to complete and re-establish connectivity. You will lose your internet connection during this time.
modemReboot.rebootWait: Please wait while we restart your modem…

supportScreen.title: We're here to help
supportScreen.subtitlePositive: Choose how you'd like to get in touch with our support team.

chatFlow.title: Chat with support
chatFlow.subtitle: Before you start, here's what to expect.
chatFlow.notLiveChat: Our chat is not a live chat — responses may be delayed. An advisor will reply as soon as possible.
chatFlow.speedUp: To speed things up, we've prepared a summary of your issue that you can paste into the chat.
chatFlow.greeting: Hello Vijay 👋 How can BASE help you today?
chatFlow.botReply: Thank you for reaching out. I'll assign you to the next available advisor who can help resolve this. Please hold on for a moment. 🙏

chatPrompt.positiveNoIssue: Hi, I don't have an issue at the moment but your scan indicates my modem may be offline. Can you help?
chatPrompt.neutralModemOffline: Hi, I ran the internet self-diagnosis tool and it detected that my modem appears to be offline. Could you please help me get back online?
chatPrompt.negativeModemOffline: Hi, I'm experiencing problems with my internet. Your self-diagnosis tool confirms that my modem is offline, which matches what I'm going through. Could you please help me resolve this?
chatPrompt.positiveWithIssue: Hi, I don't have an issue at the moment but your scan indicates I may have {{issue}}. Can you help?
chatPrompt.neutralWithIssue: Hi, I ran the internet self-diagnosis tool and your scan detected {{issue}}. Could you please help me look into it?
chatPrompt.neutralNoIssue: Hi, I ran the internet self-diagnosis tool and no issues were detected, but I'm not sure if everything is working as expected. Could you please look into it?
chatPrompt.negativeWithIssue: Hi, I'm experiencing problems with my internet. Your self-diagnosis tool detected {{issue}}, which matches what I'm going through. Could you please help me resolve this?
chatPrompt.negativeNoIssue: Hi, I'm experiencing problems with my internet but your self-diagnosis tool didn't detect any issues. Could you please help me troubleshoot further?

success.title: You're all set!
success.subtitle: Your issue has been resolved. Enjoy your internet!

clarification.title: What kind of issue?
clarification.subtitle: Help us narrow down the problem.

misalignment.title: Let's try again
misalignment.subtitle: We may not have identified the right issue. What best describes your problem?

wifiFlow.title: WiFi troubleshooting
wifiFlow.subtitlePositive: Let's try a few things to improve your WiFi:

modemDeregs.title: Poor or no connectivity
modemDeregs.subtitle: We detect that you may have poor or no connectivity. The most probable cause is a malfunctioning modem caused by a signal issue.
modemDeregs.restartModemDesc: A reboot can often resolve signal issues with your modem.

dropcable.title: Poor or no connectivity
dropcable.subtitle: We detect that you may have poor or no connectivity. The most probable cause is a cabling issue.
dropcable.tightenCableDesc: Make sure the coax cable is firmly screwed into the back of your modem.

technician.causeSubtitle: We detect that you may have poor or no connectivity. The most probable cause is a network issue outside your home.
technician.resolutionSubtitle: This issue requires an on-site inspection by one of our technicians. Please contact us to schedule a visit.

brokenHardware.causeSubtitle: We detect that you have poor or no connectivity. The most probable cause is a defective modem.
brokenHardware.resolutionSubtitle: Your modem needs to be replaced. Please contact our support team to arrange a replacement.

modem_deregs.label: Local network issue
modem_deregs.description: Your modem has been experiencing signal deregistrations — brief moments where it loses sync with the network. This can cause intermittent drops or slowdowns.

dropcable.label: Cable signal issue
dropcable.description: We've detected signal degradation on the cable connecting your modem. This could be caused by a loose or damaged coaxial cable.

coverage.label: WiFi coverage issue
coverage.description: Parts of your home may have weak WiFi signal. This is usually caused by distance from the router, walls, or interference from other devices.

filter.label: Technical issue
filter.description: A filter component in your connection needs attention. This requires a technician visit to resolve.

filter_hp47.label: Technical issue
filter_hp47.description: A specific filter component (HP47) has been flagged. A technician visit is required to inspect and replace it.

filter_tof.label: Technical issue
filter_tof.description: A filter issue (TOF) has been detected in your line. This needs an on-site technician to resolve.

dice.label: Technical issue
dice.description: A network component issue has been identified that requires professional attention from a technician.

broken_hardware_modem.label: Defective modem
broken_hardware_modem.description: Our diagnostics indicate your modem may have a hardware fault. It might need to be replaced to ensure stable connectivity.

coverage.boosterQuestion: Do you have a WiFi booster installed in your home?

interference.subtitle: The most probable cause is battling WiFi signals — your modem and booster are both broadcasting, which can create interference.
interference.solutionsSubtitle: Your modem WiFi and booster may be competing for signal. Turning off the modem's WiFi lets your booster handle everything — reducing interference.
interference.turnOffDesc: Let your booster manage the wireless signal to avoid interference.
interference.turningOffWait: Please wait while we turn off your modem's WiFi radio…
interference.verifySubtitle: Did turning off the modem WiFi improve your experience?

poorBooster.subtitle: The most probable cause is poor booster placement — your modem WiFi is off, so coverage depends entirely on your booster's reach.
poorBooster.solutionsSubtitle: Your booster might be too far from the modem, or it may not be strong enough to cover your whole home.
poorBooster.repositionDesc: Move it closer to your modem or to a central location in your home.

general.subtitle: The most probable cause is a WiFi coverage issue — your modem's signal may not reach all areas of your home.
general.solutionsSubtitle: Your modem's WiFi signal may not be strong enough to reach every room. Here's what can help.
general.repositionDesc: Move it to a central, elevated spot away from walls and electronics.

diagnosticPanel.title: 🛠 Diagnostic Input Panel
diagnosticPanel.selectSentiment: Select a sentiment below to run diagnosis
